title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline
text: OptSLDP optimized extended R implementation Selective Linkage Disequilibrium (LD) Pruning pipeline genomic prediction panel construction. package builds algorithm Zhu et al. (2023), demonstrated preserving markers strong LD statistically important SNPs substantially improves genomic prediction accuracy compared standard uniform LD pruning. OptSLDP extends work four concrete algorithmic improvements described detail .

what: Why standard LD pruning is not enough for genomic prediction
title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline
text: Standard LD pruning (e.g., PLINK --indep-pairwise) removes one marker every highly correlated pair without regard whether either marker associated trait interest. QTL region contains cluster correlated markers, one discarded — yet retained singleton may best proxy causal variant, statistical evidence region reduced. SLDP addresses partitioning marker set two groups pruning: Important SNPs (\(\mathcal{}\)): SNPs statistically associated least one trait, plus markers strong LD within positional window. retained full. Background SNPs (\(\mathcal{B} = \Omega \setminus \mathcal{}\)): remainder, receives standard greedy LD pruning control redundancy. final panel \(\mathcal{} \cup \text{retained}(\mathcal{B})\).

(VCF) Two traits independent genetic architectures: partial overlap Trait1 Trait2 across LD blocks (C), combined Trait2’s unique singleton QTN SNP020, tests whether union-protection logic correctly retains SNP020 even though never candidate Trait1 alone.

title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline
text: standard format columns SNP, CHR, POS, REF, ALT followed one column per sample. Values must \(\{0, 1, 2, \text{NA}\}\). files exceeding 200 000 rows, reader automatically switches two-pass chunked strategy: Pass 1 — header-fread(nrows = 0) determine column names count rows. Pre-allocation — matrix(NA_real_, nrow, ncol) allocated . Pass 2 — data filled 50 000-row chunks; chunk released gc(FALSE) called reading next. threshold chunk size configurable:
code: geno_obj <- read_numeric_genotype(geno_file)  # Structure of the returned list str(geno_obj, max.level = 1) #> List of 4 #>  $ snp_info  :Classes 'data.table' and 'data.frame': 40 obs. of  5 variables: #>   ..- attr(*, ".internal.selfref")=<externalptr>  #>  $ geno_mat  : num [1:40, 1:50] 1 0 0 1 0 0 0 0 NA 1 ... #>   ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>  $ sample_ids: chr [1:50] "Line01" "Line02" "Line03" "Line04" ... #>  $ format    : chr "numeric"  # SNP metadata head(geno_obj$snp_info) #>       SNP    CHR   POS    REF    ALT #>    <char> <char> <int> <char> <char> #> 1: SNP001      1 10000      A      T #> 2: SNP002      1 20000      A      T #> 3: SNP003      1 30000      A      G #> 4: SNP004      1 40000      A      G #> 5: SNP005      1 50000      C      G #> 6: SNP006      1 60000      T      C  # Dimensions of the genotype matrix (SNPs × samples) dim(geno_obj$geno_mat) #> [1] 40 50  # Sample IDs head(geno_obj$sample_ids) #> [1] "Line01" "Line02" "Line03" "Line04" "Line05" "Line06" geno_obj <- read_numeric_genotype(   file            = "large_panel.csv",   chunk_rows      = 50000L,    # rows per chunk (default)   chunk_threshold = 200000L    # rows above which chunked reading is used )

title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline
text: screening, near-duplicate SNPs (\(r^2 \ge \tau_{\text{pre}}\), default 0.99) removed chromosome--chromosome greedy forward-selection algorithm. step targets perfect near-perfect redundancy (duplicate probes, collapsed haplotypes) without removing biological LD signal. end chromosome’s loop, \(r^2\) matrix freed immediately gc(FALSE) called, preventing heap fragmentation accumulating across chromosomes:
code: pre_res <- preprune_high_ld(   snp_info     = maf_res$snp_info,   geno_mat     = maf_res$geno_mat,   r2_pre       = 0.99,   verbose      = FALSE )  cat("SNPs before pre-pruning:", nrow(maf_res$snp_info), "\n") #> SNPs before pre-pruning: 40 cat("SNPs after  pre-pruning:", nrow(pre_res$snp_info), "\n") #> SNPs after  pre-pruning: 40 # Internal logic in preprune_high_ld() and prune_background_snps() r2_mat <- compute_r2_subset(geno_mat, chr_snps) # ... pruning loop ... rm(r2_mat, kept) gc(FALSE)   # explicit release before the next chromosome

title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline
text: SNP \(\), marginal linear regression fitted: \[y^* = \alpha + \beta_i\, g_i + \varepsilon, \quad \varepsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2 )\] \(y^*\) working phenotype (see covariate adjustment ), \(g_i\) dosage vector SNP \(\), \(\alpha\), \(\beta_i\) intercept effect size. Covariate adjustment. covariates \(X_c\) supplied, phenotype residualised SNP loop: \[y^* = M_{X_c}\, y = \left[- X_c(X_c^\top X_c)^{-1} X_c^\top\right] y\] equivalent fitting \(y \sim X_c + g_i\) every SNP avoids refitting covariates regression, giving \(O(n)\) saving \(O(n \cdot p)\). Reported statistics per SNP: SNPs fewer 5 complete observations zero variance receive NA statistical columns.

title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline
text: Three modes map screening statistics candidate set \(\mathcal{C}\): Mode — p-value threshold \[\mathcal{C}_A = \{: p_i \le \tau_p\}\] Mode B — effect-size criteria \[\mathcal{C}_B = \{: |z_i| \ge \tau_z \;\text{[/]}\; R^2_i \ge \tau_{\text{pve}}\}\] Mode C — hybrid \[\mathcal{C}_C = \{: p_i \le \tau_p \;\text{[/]}\; |z_i| \ge \tau_z\}\]
code:
cands_A <- select_candidate_snps(   screen_stats,   mode           = "A",   pval_threshold = 0.05 ) cat("Mode A candidates:", length(cands_A), "
") #> Mode A candidates: 7 cands_A #> [1] "SNP003" "SNP006" "SNP007" "SNP008" "SNP009" "SNP013" "SNP027" # OR logic: keep SNPs with large z OR meaningful PVE cands_B <- select_candidate_snps(   screen_stats,   mode          = "B",   z_threshold   = 2.0,   pve_threshold = 0.05,   logic         = "OR" ) cat("Mode B (OR) candidates:", length(cands_B), "
") #> Mode B (OR) candidates: 15  # AND logic: require both criteria simultaneously cands_B_and <- select_candidate_snps(   screen_stats,   mode          = "B",   z_threshold   = 2.0,   pve_threshold = 0.05,   logic         = "AND" ) cat("Mode B (AND) candidates:", length(cands_B_and), "
") #> Mode B (AND) candidates: 8 cands_C <- select_candidate_snps(   screen_stats,   mode           = "C",   pval_threshold = 0.05,   z_threshold    = 1.5,   logic          = "OR" ) cat("Mode C (OR) candidates:", length(cands_C), "
") #> Mode C (OR) candidates: 15

title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline
text: Candidate SNPs expanded protected set \(\mathcal{}\) two layers: Layer 1 — positional window: \[\mathcal{W}(c) = \{: \text{chr}() = \text{chr}(c),\; |\text{pos}() - \text{pos}(c)| \le w\}\] \(w = w_{\text{kb}} \times 1000\) bp (default \(w = 50\,000\) bp side). Layer 2 — LD neighbours (optional): \[\mathcal{L}(c) = \{\\mathcal{W}(c) : r^2(, c) \ge \tau_{\text{flag}}\}\] protected set union candidates: \[\mathcal{} = \bigcup_{c \\mathcal{C}} \left[\mathcal{W}(c) \cup \mathcal{L}(c)\right]\] \(r^2\) statistic squared Pearson correlation computed dosage matrix pairwise-complete observations: \[r^2(, j) = \left[\frac{\text{Cov}(g_i, g_j)}{\sqrt{\text{Var}(g_i)\, \text{Var}(g_j)}}\right]^2\]
code:
important <- expand_important_snps(   candidate_snps       = cands_A,   snp_info             = snp_info_filt,   geno_mat             = geno_mat_filt,   window_kb            = 50,   include_ld_neighbors = TRUE,   r2_flag              = 0.90,   verbose              = FALSE )  cat("Candidate SNPs:  ", length(cands_A),   "
") #> Candidate SNPs:   7 cat("Important SNPs:  ", length(important), "
") #> Important SNPs:   29 cat("Added by expansion:", length(important) - length(cands_A), "

title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline
text: SNPs outside \(\mathcal{}\) form background set \(\mathcal{B} = \Omega \setminus \mathcal{}\). greedy forward-selection algorithm applied chromosome--chromosome genomic position order: \[\text{SNP } \text{ } \mathcal{B}_{\text{chr}}: \quad \text{discard } j > \text{ } r^2(, j) \ge \tau_{\text{genome}}\] default genome-wide pruning threshold \(\tau_{\text{genome}} = 0.80\). final panel combines sets:
code:
remaining <- setdiff(snp_info_filt$SNP, important) cat("Background SNPs to prune:", length(remaining), "
") #> Background SNPs to prune: 11  bg_retained <- prune_background_snps(   remaining_snps = remaining,   snp_info       = snp_info_filt,   geno_mat       = geno_mat_filt,   r2_genome      = 0.80,   verbose        = FALSE )  cat("Background retained after pruning:", length(bg_retained), "
") #> Background retained after pruning: 11 final_snps <- unique(c(important, bg_retained)) cat("Total final panel:", length(final_snps), "SNPs
") #> Total final panel: 40 SNPs  # Are known QTN in the final panel? cat("
Trait1 QTN retained?", all(qtn1 %in% final_snps), "
") #>  #> Trait1 QTN retained? TRUE cat("QTN1 IDs:", paste(qtn1[qtn1 %in% final_snps], collapse = ", "), "
") #> QTN1 IDs: SNP003, SNP015, SNP025

what: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: OptSLDP optimized extended implementation Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning genomic prediction panel construction. core idea SLDP — preserving markers strong LD statistically important SNPs genomic models retain full genetic signal around QTL, pruning redundant background markers — retained original algorithm Zhu et al. (2023). OptSLDP improves foundation four concrete ways: Memory-safe chunked reading — genotype files read fixed-size row chunks pre-allocated output matrix, peak RAM proportional one chunk rather twice full file. Explicit per-chromosome garbage collection — gc(FALSE) called chromosome’s LD matrix released pre-pruning background-pruning loops, preventing heap fragmentation across 20–30 chromosome passes. Multi-trait union protection — screening candidate selection run independently trait; union per-trait candidate sets expanded protected, every SNP important trait retained final panel. Covariate-adjusted marginal screening — phenotypes residualised shared covariates SNP scan (equivalent including covariates every marginal regression computed single lm() call), correctly adjusts effect estimates population structure confounders. OptSLDP also inspired genome-wide association study-derived marker weighting approach Akohoue et al. (2026) blast resistance prediction rice, scales automatically small -memory panels whole-genome sequencing datasets 10 million SNPs.

title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: Numeric dosage format — one row per SNP, columns SNP, CHR, POS, REF, ALT followed one column per sample, values {0, 1, 2, NA}: HapMap format — standard 11-column header (rs#, alleles, chrom, pos, strand, assembly#, center, protLSID, assayLSID, panelLSID, QCcode) followed sample columns containing two-character nucleotide calls (AA, , TT, NN missing). VCF — standard VCF v4.2. phased (0|1) unphased (0/1) GT fields accepted. Multi-allelic sites use first ALT allele. Missing calls (./.) become NA. Chromosome names without chr prefix (chr1 vs 1) normalised automatically read time.

title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: delimited file one row per sample. Required columns: Sample alignment handled internally. Samples present genotype file absent phenotype file cause informative error. Extra samples phenotype file dropped message.

title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: ALT allele frequency estimated dosage matrix : \[\text{AF}_i = \frac{\sum_j g_{ij}}{2 n_j}\] \(g_{ij} \\{0, 1, 2, \text{NA}\}\) dosage SNP \(\) sample \(j\), \(n_j\) number non-missing samples SNP. minor allele frequency : \[\text{MAF}_i = \min\!\left(\text{AF}_i,\; 1 - \text{AF}_i\right)\] SNPs \(\text{MAF}_i < \tau_{\text{maf}}\) (default 0.05) removed analysis.

title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: SNP \(\) simple marginal linear regression fitted: \[y^* = \alpha + \beta_i\, g_i + \varepsilon\] \(y^*\) working phenotype (see ), \(g_i\) dosage vector SNP \(\), \(\varepsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2 )\). Covariate adjustment. covariates \(X_c\) supplied, \(y\) first projected onto covariate space residuals used working phenotype: \[y^* = y - X_c\!\left(X_c^\top X_c\right)^{-1} X_c^\top y = M_{X_c}\, y\] \(M_{X_c}\) residual maker (annihilator) matrix. equivalent fitting \(y \sim X_c + g_i\) reading coefficient \(g_i\), residualisation performed SNP loop rather re-fitting covariates every SNP. Reported statistics per SNP: SNPs fewer 5 complete observations zero variance receive NA statistical columns.

title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: Three modes control SNPs enter protected set: Mode — p-value threshold \[\mathcal{C}_A = \left\{: p_i \le \tau_p\right\}\] Requires pval_threshold. Appropriate clear significance cutoff exists (e.g., Bonferroni permutation threshold). Mode B — effect-size criteria \[\mathcal{C}_B = \left\{: |z_i| \ge \tau_z \;\text{[/]}\; R^2_i \ge \tau_{\text{pve}}\right\}\] Requires least one z_threshold pve_threshold. threshold_logic argument ("" "") controls multiple criteria combined. Use "" capture SNPs large effects regardless significance, "" require criteria simultaneously. Mode C — hybrid \[\mathcal{C}_C = \left\{: p_i \le \tau_p \;\text{[/]}\; |z_i| \ge \tau_z \;\text{[/]}\; R^2_i \ge \tau_{\text{pve}}\right\}\] combination three criteria, combined threshold_logic.

title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: candidate set \(\mathcal{C}\) expanded full protected set \(\mathcal{}\) two layers candidate \(c\): Layer 1 — positional window. Every SNP chromosome within \(\pm w\) bp \(c\) added: \[\mathcal{W}(c) = \left\{: \text{chr}() = \text{chr}(c),\; |\text{pos}() - \text{pos}(c)| \le w\right\}\] \(w = w_{\text{kb}} \times 1000\) bp (default \(w_{\text{kb}} = 50\), \(w = 50\,000\) bp side). Layer 2 — LD neighbours (optional). Among window SNPs \(\mathcal{W}(c)\), pairwise \(r^2\) focal candidate \(c\) \(\tau_{\text{flag}}\) (default 0.90) also added: \[\mathcal{L}(c) = \left\{\\mathcal{W}(c) : r^2(, c) \ge \tau_{\text{flag}}\right\}\] full protected set union candidates: \[\mathcal{} = \bigcup_{c \\mathcal{C}} \left[\mathcal{W}(c) \cup \mathcal{L}(c)\right]\] \(r^2\) statistic used squared Pearson correlation computed dosage matrix pairwise-complete observations: \[r^2(, j) = \left[\frac{\text{Cov}(g_i, g_j)}{\sqrt{\text{Var}(g_i)\,\text{Var}(g_j)}}\right]^2\] GDS strategy correlation computed snpgdsLDMat() SNPRelate package, streams genotypes disk returns statistic.

title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: SNPs outside \(\mathcal{}\) form background set \(\mathcal{B} = \Omega \setminus \mathcal{}\) \(\Omega\) full post-filter SNP set. greedy forward-selection algorithm applied chromosome--chromosome, processing SNPs genomic position order: default genome-wide pruning threshold \(\tau_{\text{genome}} = 0.80\). final retained panel : \[\text{Panel} = \mathcal{} \;\cup\; \text{retained}(\mathcal{B})\] GDS strategy background pruning uses snpgdsPruneLD() SNPRelate, processes chromosome streaming blocks without loading full \(r^2\) matrix. equivalent threshold passed snpgdsPruneLD \(|r| \ge \sqrt{\tau_{\text{genome}}}\) function operates correlation coefficient rather \(r^2\).
code: For each chromosome:   Sort background SNPs by position   For i = 1, 2, …, |B_chr|:     If SNP i is still marked "keep":       Mark as "discard" every SNP j > i with r²(i, j) ≥ τ_genome

title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: trait_col character vector length > 1, pipeline runs separate screening every trait takes union per-trait candidate sets expansion pruning: trait \(k \\{1, \ldots, K\}\): Residualise \(y_k\) shared covariates independently. Run full marginal scan → per-trait statistics \((\hat\beta^{(k)}, p^{(k)}, R^{2(k)})\). Apply threshold criteria → per-trait candidate set \(\mathcal{C}^{(k)}\). Form union candidate set: \[\mathcal{C}^* = \bigcup_{k=1}^{K} \mathcal{C}^{(k)}\] Expand \(\mathcal{C}^*\) protected set \(\mathcal{}^*\) using window LD-neighbour rules (§4). Prune background \(\Omega \setminus \mathcal{}^*\) (§5). guarantees every SNP important trait retained, background pruning performed shared remaining set. return value multi-trait mode includes: - $screening_stats — named list per-trait data.tables. - $candidate_snps_per_trait — named list per-trait candidate vectors. - $candidate_snps — union across traits.
code: # Access per-trait results res$screening_stats$Trait1 res$screening_stats$Trait2 res$candidate_snps_per_trait$Trait2

title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: pipeline selects scale strategy automatically based number SNPs MAF filtering. strategy governs \(r^2\) matrices computed throughout pipeline. Override scale_strategy = "in_memory" / "chunked" / "gds". Adjust automatic thresholds site-wide: GDS setup large panels. GDS strategy writes genotype matrix binary SNPRelate GDS file start pipeline. subsequent LD queries stream genotypes disk blocks — full matrix never lives RAM simultaneously. GDS file handles tracked package-level registry closed exit error.
code: options(   optsldp.thresh_small  = 1e5,   # below this: in_memory   optsldp.thresh_medium = 1e6    # below this: chunked; above: gds ) # Force GDS strategy and specify a persistent scratch directory res <- run_sldp(   genotype_file  = "wgs_panel.vcf.gz",   phenotype_file = "phenotype.csv",   output_file    = "wgs_pruned.csv",   format         = "vcf",   scale_strategy = "gds",   gds_dir        = "/scratch/my_gds",   n_cores        = 16 )

title: OptSLDP — Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning
text: 12 steps executed run_sldp() order: Step 5 — high-LD pre-pruning. screening, near-duplicate SNPs (\(r^2 \ge \tau_{\text{preprune}}\), default 0.99) removed chromosome--chromosome. reduces screening workload substantially dense arrays without discarding independent signal. pre-pruning threshold \(\tau_{\text{preprune}}\) set close 1 — intended catch perfect near-perfect redundancy (e.g., duplicate probes, SNPs complete disequilibrium) rather biological LD. Detailed example parameters:
code: res <- run_sldp(   # ── Files ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   genotype_file        = "panel.hmp.txt",   phenotype_file       = "phenotype.csv",   output_file          = "panel_pruned.hmp.txt",    # ── Phenotype columns ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────   sample_col           = "Line",           # sample ID column name   trait_col            = c("YLD", "HT"),   # two traits: union protection   covar_cols           = c("PC1", "PC2"),  # shared covariates    # ── I/O formats ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   format               = "hapmap",   output_format        = "hapmap",    # ── Scale ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   scale_strategy       = NULL,             # auto-detect   gds_dir              = tempdir(),   n_cores              = 4L,    # ── Quality control ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   maf_threshold        = 0.05,   preprune_large       = TRUE,   preprune_r2          = 0.99,    # ── Candidate selection (Mode C: p-value OR large z) ───────────────────────   mode                 = "C",   pval_threshold       = 0.001,   z_threshold          = 3.5,   threshold_logic      = "OR",    # ── Important SNP protection ───────────────────────────────────────────────   window_kb            = 50,   include_ld_neighbors = TRUE,   r2_flag              = 0.90,    # ── Background pruning ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────   r2_genome            = 0.80,   slide_max_bp         = 1000000L,    # ── Reporting ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   stats_output_file    = "pruning_stats.csv",   summary_output_file  = "pruning_summary.txt",   verbose              = TRUE )

Approximately 5% values missing random. Row names SNP IDs; column names sample IDs. genotype matrix also provided three file formats inst/extdata/. sample_ids Character vector 50 sample IDs (Line01 Line50). Matches colnames(geno_mat) Sample column pheno_df. trait1 Numeric vector length 50. Simulated phenotype Trait1 (blast resistance proxy, heritability \(h^2 = 0.55\)). QTN: SNP003 (+0.80), SNP015 (-0.50), SNP025 (+0.65). trait2 Numeric vector length 50. Simulated phenotype Trait2 (heritability \(h^2 = 0.45\)). QTN: SNP006 (+0.70), SNP028 (-0.60), SNP020 (+0.45). SNP020 singleton outside LD blocks, specifically included test multi-trait union-protection run_sldp(). pheno_matrix Numeric matrix (50 samples x 2 traits) column names Trait1 Trait2 row names matching sample_ids. Pass directly compute_screening_stats() run_sldp() via trait_col = c("Trait1", "Trait2"). covariates Data frame (50 rows x 2 columns: PC1, PC2). Simulated principal-component covariates. Pass run_sldp() via covar_cols = c("PC1", "PC2"). pheno_df Data frame (50 rows x 5 columns: Sample, Trait1, Trait2, PC1, PC2). complete phenotype table; matches inst/extdata/example_phenotype.csv exactly. gwas_trait1 Data frame columns SNP, CHR, POS, beta, SE, P.value. Pre-computed marginal OLS results Trait1, sorted ascending p-value. diagnostic use ; required run_sldp(). gwas_trait2 Data frame structure gwas_trait1 Trait2. qtn_trait1 Data frame columns SNP effect. Ground-truth causal variants Trait1 (SNP003, SNP015, SNP025). testing – unknown real analyses. qtn_trait2 Data frame columns SNP effect. Ground-truth causal variants Trait2 (SNP006, SNP028, SNP020). testing – unknown real analyses. genotype_numeric Data frame representation genotype matrix standard numeric-dosage format (columns: SNP, CHR, POS, REF, ALT, one column per sample). Matches inst/extdata/example_genotypes_numeric.csv exactly. description Character scalar. Plain-text summary dataset design key parameters. n_snps Integer scalar. Number SNPs (40). n_samples Integer scalar. Number samples (50). seed Integer scalar. Random seed used generate dataset (42L). what: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline — OptSLDP-package
title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline — OptSLDP-package
text: OptSLDP implements optimized extended version Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning (SLDP) pipeline genomic prediction panel construction. package builds algorithm Zhu et al. (2023) addresses four concrete limitations original implementation: memory-safe chunked genotype reading, explicit per-chromosome garbage collection, multi-trait union protection, covariate-adjusted marginal screening.

title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline — OptSLDP-package
text: Standard LD pruning (e.g., PLINK --indep-pairwise) removes markers correlated pairs without regard whether either marker associated trait. discards statistical evidence around QTL regions. SLDP addresses partitioning marker set pruning: Important SNPs markers statistically associated least one trait, plus markers strong LD within positional window. retained full. Background SNPs remainder. Standard greedy LD pruning applied subset control redundancy. final panel union important SNPs retained background markers.

title: OptSLDP: An Optimized Selective Linkage Disequilibrium Pruning Pipeline — OptSLDP-package

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: read_genotype() — auto-dispatch wrapper detects genotype file format file extension (.vcf / .vcf.gz → VCF; .hmp.txt → HapMap; everything else → numeric dosage) calls appropriate format-specific reader. read_numeric_genotype() — reads files package’s standard tabular format (SNP, CHR, POS, REF, ALT, sample1, sample2, ..., values 0/1/2/NA). Files chunk_threshold rows (default 200 000) processed two-pass chunked strategy: header-scan pre-allocates full output matrix, data filled chunk_rows-row slices (default 50 000) peak RAM equals one chunk rather twice full file. read_hapmap_genotype() — reads standard HapMap format, converting two-character nucleotide calls ("AA", "", "TT", "NN") additive dosage (0/1/2/NA). Uses two-pass chunked strategy read_numeric_genotype() large files. read_vcf_genotype() — reads VCF v4.2 files (plain bgzipped) via VariantAnnotation. Accepts phased (0|1) unphased (0/1) GT fields. Multi-allelic sites use first ALT allele; missing calls (./.) become NA. Requires Bioconductor packages VariantAnnotation, GenomeInfoDb, SummarizedExperiment, Biostrings, S4Vectors.

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: read_phenotype() — reads delimited phenotype file, extracts one trait columns optional shared covariates, aligns rows genotype sample order. single trait column returns plain numeric vector (backward compatible); character vector column names returns named numeric matrix (n_samples × n_traits) multi-trait analysis. Duplicate sample IDs, mismatched sample sets, non-numeric trait values produce informative errors warnings.

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: write_numeric_genotype() — writes pruned genotype matrix SNP metadata standard SNP, CHR, POS, REF, ALT, sample... CSV format. write_hapmap_genotype() — converts pruned dosage matrix back two-character nucleotide calls writes tab-delimited HapMap file. write_pruning_report() — writes step--step pruning statistics data.table CSV file, optionally writes human-readable plain-text summary showing SNP counts every pipeline step.

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: compute_maf() — computes ALT allele frequency (AF) minor allele frequency (MAF) every SNP dosage matrix. AF estimated rowMeans(geno_mat, na.rm = TRUE) / 2; MAF min(AF, 1 - AF). Scale-aware: delegates snpgdsSNPRateFreq() GDS context active. filter_snps_by_maf() — removes SNPs whose MAF maf_threshold (default 0.05) NA. Returns filtered snp_info, geno_mat, MAF table. Scale-aware. preprune_high_ld() — chromosome-wise greedy removal near-duplicate SNPs high r² threshold (default 0.99) main pipeline. Intended catch collapsed haplotypes duplicate probes, biological LD. Scale-aware. Explicit rm() + gc(FALSE) chromosome’s r² matrix releases allocator pressure across chromosome passes.

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: compute_screening_stats() — fits marginal linear regression phenotype SNP. covariates supplied, phenotype residualised scan (equivalent including covariates every regression computed single lm() call). Returns per-SNP beta, SE, z_score, P.value, PVE, AF, MAF. Accepts numeric vector (single trait) numeric matrix (multi-trait), returning single data.table named list data.tables respectively. select_candidate_snps() — applies one three selection modes screening statistics table: Mode — p-value threshold: p ≤ τ_p Mode B — effect-size criteria: |z| ≥ τ_z /R² ≥ τ_pve, combined "" "" logic Mode C — hybrid: combination modes B criteria

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: compute_r2_subset() — computes pairwise r² matrix SNP subset. -memory / chunked path uses cor() pairwise-complete observations; GDS path uses snpgdsLDMat() streams genotypes disk. Scale-aware. find_ld_neighbors() — returns IDs SNPs candidate set whose pairwise r² focal SNP meets exceeds r2_threshold. Used internally important-SNP expansion. Scale-aware. expand_important_snps() — expands candidate set protected set two layers: (1) SNPs within ±window_kb kb chromosome, optionally, SNPs window r² ≥ r2_flag focal candidate. protected set union candidates. Scale-aware. prune_background_snps() — greedy forward-selection LD pruning SNPs outside protected set, applied chromosome--chromosome genomic position order. Discards SNP j > r²(, j) ≥ r2_genome (default 0.80). Explicit rm() + gc(FALSE) chromosome’s r² matrix. Scale-aware; GDS path delegates snpgdsPruneLD().

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: run_sldp() accepts trait_col character vector length. multiple traits specified: Marginal screening candidate selection run independently trait (separate phenotype residualisation, separate p-values, separate candidate sets). union per-trait candidate sets formed: C* = C_1 ∪ C_2 ∪ ... ∪ C_K. union set expanded protected set background pruning runs remainder. Every SNP important trait therefore retained final panel. return value carries per-trait screening statistics ($screening_stats, named list) per-trait candidate vectors ($candidate_snps_per_trait, named list) alongside shared final panel.

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: Genotype files exceeding chunk_threshold rows (default 200 000) read two-pass strategy. Pass 1 reads header determine dimensions pre-allocates matrix(NA_real_, nrow, ncol). Pass 2 reads chunk_rows (default 50 000) rows time writes block directly pre-allocated matrix slice. Peak RAM equals one chunk (~400 MB 50 K × 3 K panel) rather twice file size (standard fread + .matrix path). gc(FALSE) called chunk released.

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: gc(FALSE) called every chromosome’s r² matrix released preprune_high_ld() prune_background_snps(). prevents allocator fragmentation accumulating across 20–30 chromosome passes — issue causes apparent memory leaks large panels without explicitly triggering collection passes.

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: pipeline selects one three LD backends based post-filter SNP count, site-wide adjustable thresholds: Thresholds configurable via options(optsldp.thresh_small, optsldp.thresh_medium). GDS backend writes genotype matrix binary SNPRelate GDS file , streams LD queries disk, closes file handles automatically via .exit() package-level handle registry.

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: three genotype readers normalise chromosome names read time stripping leading "chr" prefix (case-insensitive), "chr1" "1" treated chromosome throughout pipeline without requiring manual harmonisation.

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: covar_cols specified, phenotype projected onto covariate space SNP scan: y* = y - X_c (X_c' X_c)^{-1} X_c' y equivalent including covariates every marginal regression avoids refitting covariates thousands SNPs, giving O(n_covariates) saving naïve approach.

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: package ships five files inst/extdata/ encoding underlying 40-SNP × 50-sample synthetic dataset supported formats: example_genotypes_numeric.csv — numeric dosage (0/1/2/NA) example_genotypes.hmp.txt — HapMap nucleotide calls example_genotypes.vcf — VCF v4.2; SNP001–SNP010 phased (|), SNP011–SNP040 unphased (/) example_phenotype.csv — two traits (Trait1, Trait2) two covariates (PC1, PC2) 50 samples example_gwas.csv — pre-computed GWAS results Trait1 (diagnostic ; required run_sldp()) dataset known genetic architecture designed exercise pipeline branches: Three LD blocks: Block (SNP001–SNP008, chr1, r² ≈ 0.80), Block B (SNP012–SNP018, chr1, r² ≈ 0.60), Block C (SNP023–SNP030, chr2, r² ≈ 0.80) ~5% missing values across three file formats Trait1 QTN: SNP003 (+0.80), SNP015 (−0.50), SNP025 (+0.65); h² = 0.55; one QTN per LD block Trait2 QTN: SNP006 (+0.70), SNP028 (−0.60), SNP020 (+0.45); h² = 0.45; SNP020 singleton outside LD blocks, specifically test union-protection retains via Trait2 candidate set

title: OptSLDP 0.1.0
text: data-raw/generate_example_data.R — reproducible script (seed 42) generates five inst/extdata/ files data/example_optsldp.rda binary dataset first principles, 22 self-consistency assertions. vignettes/OptSLDP-introduction.Rmd — full introductory vignette covering functions, three candidate selection modes, single-trait multi-trait workflows, scale strategies, output writers, memory notes; includes 43 named code chunks 14 LaTeX display-math blocks. pkgdown/extra_head.html — custom <head> content pkgdown site: Google Fonts preconnect hints, Open Graph / Twitter Card meta tags, MathJax 3 delimiter configuration, bslib-aligned CSS refinements, copy--clipboard button code blocks. _pkgdown.yml — Bootstrap 5 pkgdown site configuration Inter / Fira Code fonts, navy--blue colour scheme, five-section function reference index. .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml — GitHub Actions workflow installs Bioconductor dependencies, builds pkgdown site build_site_github_pages(), deploys GitHub Pages via actions/deploy-pages@v4.
